title: Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 5.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.54: Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.54' running on pan:globalprotect config fields if logged, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval split=coalesce(split_tunnel,"unknown")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT tunnel_mode_required
| where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"
| stats dc(public_ip) as distinct_exit, count by user, portal, split
description: Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vpn and sourcetypes pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Push portal config from Panorama; (2) block local subnet access lists; (3) monthly config export diff; (4) user re-education. Indexes required: `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`; Lookups: `ot_vpn_profiles`, `ot_vpn_profiles.csv`.
visualization: Table, Single value (misconfigured sessions), Bar. Key fields: `distinct_exit`, `portal`, `split`, `user`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:globalprotect` config fields if logged.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push portal config from Panorama; (2) block local subnet access lists; (3) monthly config export diff; (4) user re-education.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval split=coalesce(split_tunnel,"unknown")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT tunnel_mode_required
| where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"
| stats dc(public_ip) as distinct_exit, count by user, portal, split
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal (IEC 62443-3-2 / Remote access conduit integrity)** — Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` config fields if logged. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **split** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, portal, split** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (misconfigured sessions), Bar.
grandmaExplanation: We detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
